{
}

name: owned_threads_shared_across_containers
code:
proof fn phi_3_owned_threads_shared_across_containers(
    pm: ProcessManager,
    c1: ContainerPtr,
    c2: ContainerPtr,
    t: ThreadPtr,
)
    requires
        pm.wf(),
        pm.container_dom().contains(c1),
        pm.container_dom().contains(c2),
        c1 != c2,
        pm.get_container(c1).owned_threads@.contains(t),
        pm.get_container(c2).owned_threads@.contains(t),
    ensures
        pm.get_container(c1).owned_threads@.contains(t),
        pm.get_container(c2).owned_threads@.contains(t),
{
}
reason: Neither container_perms_wf nor container_tree_wf constrains owned_threads — two distinct containers can own the same thread, violating the unique ownership model
